reasoning: The US Treasury's General Account (TGA) has a relatively high known recent balance ($905 billion on October 30, 2025), which is above the typical $750-$850B target range, suggesting a baseline bias toward draining over the near term. The first target date (2025-11-16) captures data for the Wednesday prior (November 12), which misses the large mid-month cash inflows that settle on or just after the 15th, making a decline highly probable. By December 9, comparing the early-to-mid-December TGA (often a local low before the heavy December 15 corporate tax receipts) with early November, the balance will likely still be lower, reinforcing a below-50% probability. For the final date (February 7, 2026), predicting three months out brings much higher fundamental uncertainty regarding fiscal flows, debt issuance changes, and Treasury policy targets, so my confidence pulls the prediction closer to the 50% max-entropy mark.
